description_long: This is a rustic wooden cabin with a steep, asymmetrical gable roof covered in dark, weathered shingles. The exterior walls are constructed from horizontal wooden planks, giving the structure a traditional, hand-built appearance. The cabin features several rectangular windows with a yellowish tint, distributed unevenly across the visible facades, and a single wooden door with vertical planking and a small window above it. The roof has a pronounced overhang and visible wooden rafters at the gable ends, adding to its old-world charm. The wood appears aged and mossy, suggesting the cabin has been exposed to the elements for some time. There is no visible porch or veranda, and the overall design is simple yet functional, suitable for a rural or forested urban edge setting.
description: A rustic wooden cabin with a steep, weathered gable roof, multiple yellow-tinted windows, and a single wooden door. The exterior is aged wood with visible rafters and no porch.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the main entrance door, two windows on the front facade, and the steep, shingled roof with visible rafters.
description_view_1: Left-rear view highlighting three side windows, the extended roof overhang, and the wooden plank siding.
description_view_2: Rear-right view displaying the back wall with a single window and the continuation of the shingled roof.
description_view_3: Front-right view showing the front door, a single front window, and the angled roof with exposed rafters.
attribute_roof_type: asymmetrical gable roof with steep pitch, overhanging eaves, and wooden shingles
attribute_window_arrangement: Five rectangular windows of similar size, asymmetrically distributed: two on the front, three on the left side, and one on the rear, all with a yellowish tint
attribute_door_placement: Single exterior door located on the front facade, slightly offset to the left beneath a small window
